perturbation.original_text: 1.33 "FCPA" means the U.S. Foreign Corrupt Practices Act of 1977, as amended, including the rules and regulations thereunder. A summary of the FCPA and related information can be found at http://www.justice.gov/criminal/fraud/fcpa.
perturbation.changed_text: 1.33 "FCPA" means the U.S. Foreign Corrupt Practices Act of 1977, as amended, including the rules and regulations thereunder. A summary of the FCPA and related information can optionally be found at http://www.justice.gov/criminal/fraud/fcpa.
perturbation.explanation: By changing 'can be found' to 'can optionally be found', the contract suggests that compliance resources are optional, undermining the FCPA's mandatory requirements.
perturbation.contradicted_law: U.S. Foreign Corrupt Practices Act of 1977
perturbation.law_explanation: The FCPA mandates certain record-keeping and internal control provisions. Suggesting access to compliance information is optional contradicts the law's intent to ensure mandatory compliance.
perturbation.scraped_snippet_1: Criminal Division | Foreign Corrupt Practices Act Unit Skip to main content MENU Foreign Corrupt Practices Act Unit Corporate Enforcement Policy Statutes Legislative History FCPA Guidelines FCPA Resource Guide Enforcement Actions A - M N - Z Chronological List Corporate Enforcement Actions Individual Enforcement Actions FCPA Opinions Opinion Procedure Regulations (PDF) Opinion Releases Index Summaries Opinion Procedure Releases Review Procedure Releases International Agreements Anti-Corruption Sites Foreign Corrupt Practices Act Unit Share Facebook X LinkedIn Email An Overview Foreign Corrupt Practices Act The Foreign Corrupt Practices Act of 1977, as amended, 15 U.S.C. §§ 78dd-1, et seq. ("FCPA"), was enacted for the purpose of making it unlawful for certain classes of persons and entities to make payments to foreign government officials to assist in obtaining or retaining business. Specifically, the anti-bribery provisions of the FCPA prohibit the willful use of the mails or any means of instrumentality of interstate commerce corruptly in furtherance of any offer, payment, promise to pay, or authorization of the payment of money or anything of value to any person, while knowing that all or a portion of such money or thing of value will be offered, given or promised, directly or indirectly, to a foreign official to influence the foreign official in his or her official capacity, induce the foreign official to do or omit to do an act in violation of his or her lawful duty, or to secure any improper advantage in order to assist in obtaining or retaining business for or with, or directing business to, any person. Since 1977, the anti-bribery provisions of the FCPA have applied to all U.S. persons and certain foreign issuers of securities. With the enactment of certain amendments in 1998, the anti-bribery provisions of the FCPA now also apply to foreign firms and persons who cause, directly or through agents, an act in furtherance of such a corrupt payment to take place within the territory of the United States. The FCPA also requires companies whose securities are listed in the United States to meet its accounting provisions. See 15 U.S.C. § 78m. These accounting provisions, which were designed to operate in tandem with the anti-bribery provisions of the FCPA, require corporations covered by the provisions to (a) make and keep books and records that accurately and fairly reflect the transactions of the corporation and (b) devise and maintain an adequate system of internal accounting controls. For particular FCPA compliance questions relating to specific conduct, you should seek the advice of counsel as well as consider using t
perturbation.justification: The original text states that a summary of the FCPA 'can be found' at a given link, while the modified text changes this to 'can optionally be found'. The Perturbation Explanation indicates this suggests that compliance resources are optional, which undermines the FCPA's mandatory requirements. The Law Explanation states the FCPA mandates record-keeping and internal control provisions, and making access to compliance information optional contradicts the intent to ensure mandatory compliance. Scraped Law Snippet 1 explains the FCPA was enacted to make it unlawful for certain persons to make payments to foreign government officials and requires companies to keep accurate records and maintain adequate internal accounting controls. Suggesting that information on the FCPA is optional contradicts the mandatory nature of the FCPA's compliance requirements, creating a legal contradiction under the 'Misaligned Terminology Legal Contradiction' definition.

perturbation.original_text: 1.8 "Applicable Law" means (a) any federal, state, local, foreign or multinational law, statute, standard, ordinance, code, rule, regulation, resolution or promulgation (including written governmental interpretations thereof, the guidance related thereto), (b) any judicial, governmental or administrative order, judgment, decree or ruling by any Governmental Authority, or (c) any license, franchise, permit or similar right granted under any of the foregoing, or any similar provision having the force or effect of law, in each case (a), (b) and (c) that may be in effect from time to time and as applicable to the subject matter and the Persons at issue.
perturbation.changed_text: 1.8 "Applicable Law" means (a) any federal, state, local, foreign or multinational law, statute, standard, ordinance, code, rule, regulation, resolution or promulgation (excluding written governmental interpretations thereof, the guidance related thereto), (b) any judicial, governmental or administrative order, judgment, decree or ruling by any Governmental Authority, or (c) any license, franchise, permit or similar right granted under any of the foregoing, or any similar provision having the force or effect of law, in each case (a), (b) and (c) that may be in effect from time to time and as applicable to the subject matter and the Persons at issue.
perturbation.explanation: By excluding 'written governmental interpretations thereof, the guidance related thereto,' the modified definition conflicts with the established legal principle that agency interpretations of laws are often binding and must be considered part of 'Applicable Law'.
perturbation.law_explanation: The APA requires agencies to provide notice and solicit comments on proposed rules. These rules and their interpretations (guidance) carry legal weight, and excluding them from the definition of 'Applicable Law' contradicts this principle.

perturbation.scraped_snippet_2: Page Not Found | GovInfo Skip to main content Page Not Found Help Error occurred. The page you requested cannot be found. Please report this error to askGPO. Provide the following information to help us resolve this problem: the URL of the page you were trying to access, the steps you followed to produce the error, specific search or browse terms, and/or a screenshot of the page where the error occurred. Thank you for your patience. Homepage Search Tips Skip back to top
perturbation.justification: The modified contract text, by excluding 'written governmental interpretations thereof, the guidance related thereto' from the definition of 'Applicable Law,' creates a contradiction. The Law Explanation indicates that agency interpretations and guidance have legal weight, a principle supported by the Administrative Procedure Act. Although Scraped Law Snippet 2 is a 'Page Not Found,' Scraped Law Snippet 1 references the Administrative Procedure Act, highlighting its significance in US law. Omitting governmental interpretations undermines the legally binding nature of agency guidance, which can be considered part of 'Applicable Law,' thus creating a legal contradiction under the definition of 'Misaligned Terminology Legal Contradiction' where the contract deviates from legally recognized definitions, potentially making it unenforceable.

perturbation.original_text: 1.76 "Phase 1 Clinical Trial" means a Clinical Trial of a Product that generally provides for the first introduction into humans of such Product, with the primary purpose of determining metabolism and pharmacokinetic properties and side effects of such product, in a manner that is generally consistent with 21 C.F.R. § 312.21(a), as amended (or its successor regulation), excluding, for clarity, any investigator-initiated Clinical Trials unless agreed to by the JRDC.
perturbation.changed_text: 1.76 "Phase 1 Clinical Trial" means a Clinical Trial of a Product that generally provides for the first introduction into humans of such Product, with the primary purpose of determining metabolism and pharmacokinetic properties and side effects of such product, in a manner that is loosely consistent with 21 C.F.R. § 312.21(a), as amended (or its successor regulation), excluding, for clarity, any investigator-initiated Clinical Trials unless agreed to by the JRDC.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'generally consistent' to 'loosely consistent' weakens the requirement for adherence to FDA regulations for Phase 1 trials.
perturbation.law_explanation: FDA regulations tightly govern Phase 1 trials. Loosening the consistency requirement contradicts the FDA's mandate for strict adherence to safety and protocol standards.

perturbation.justification: The change from "generally consistent" to "loosely consistent" introduces a legal contradiction because it weakens the adherence to FDA regulations for Phase 1 clinical trials. The Law Explanation states that loosening the consistency requirement contradicts the FDA's mandate for strict adherence to safety and protocol standards. Scraped Law Snippet 1 refers to 21 CFR 312.21, which governs the phases of an investigation. By making the standard "loosely consistent", the modified text deviates from the required consistency, creating a potential non-compliance issue. This falls under the 'Misaligned Terminology Legal Contradiction' definition.
